data.title: Britain will not protect Israel as Iran retaliates - Times newspaper
data.description:
Britain will not protect Israel as Iran retaliates against overnight Israeli strikes, the defence editor of the Times newspaper said on X, without citing sources.

In October 2024, when Iran fired a salvo of ballistic missiles at Israel, Britain said two of its fighter jets and an air-to-air refuelling tanker played a part in attempts to prevent further escalation, but that the jets did not engage any targets.

Britain was not involved in Israel's strikes against Iran overnight and Prime Minister Keir Starmer has urged both sides to use restraint and return to diplomacy.

Britain's foreign office and Ministry of Defence did not immediately respond to a request for comment on any potential British involvement in protecting Israel.

data.title: Neutech Affiliate to Pay Higher Land Fee as Acquired Land's Size Increases
data.description:
Neutech Group said its affiliated entity, Dalian Neusoft University of Information, signed supplementary terms to a land use rights agreement with the Dalian Natural Resources Bureau, according to a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing.

Under the revised terms, the building area of Land Parcel B in Dalian's High-tech Industrial Zone was increased to 8,800 square meters from 8,100 square meters. The land grant fee rose by 1.1 million yuan to 14.1 million yuan, payable within 30 days.

data.title: Lao Feng Xiang to Set Up Hong Kong Subsidiary for $200 Million
data.description:
Lao Feng Xiang plans to invest $200 million to establish a subsidiary in Hong Kong, according to a Shanghai bourse filing on Friday.

The subsidiary, to be named Lao Feng Xiang Hong Kong, will be a seller of gold and silver products, as well as arts and crafts products, the Chinese jewelry company said.

Lao Feng Xiang's A shares dropped over 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Inspiration Energy Engages CC Explorations to Advance Targeting on Saskatchewan Projects Using Atomic Mineral Resonance Tomography (AMRT) Technology
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - June 13, 2025) - Inspiration Energy Corp.  (WKN: A40GPX) (OTC Pink: ISPNF) ("Inspiration" or the "Company") is pleased to announce it has engaged CC Explorations, a leader in satellite-based remote sensing, to assist in identifying high-priority drill targets across its Saskatchewan exploration properties.

CC Explorations brings over 20 years of experience in remote sensing for mineral exploration and archaeological services. Their proprietary Atomic Mineral Resonance Tomography (AMRT) technology—comparable to systems used by NASA for planetary mineral detection—provides precise, non-invasive sub-surface imaging. This technology will be used to refine geological models and accelerate discovery across Inspiration's portfolio, including its Rottenstone North Gold Project, which lies along trend from Ramp Metals Inc.'s recent high-profile battery metals discovery.

"We're excited to partner with CC Explorations to enhance our exploration strategy using cutting-edge satellite imaging," said Charles Desjardins, CEO of Inspiration Energy. "This engagement is expected to significantly improve targeting efficiency and help unlock the full potential of our Saskatchewan assets. With AMRT data guiding us and gold prices near all-time highs, we are entering the summer exploration season with strong momentum."

Inspiration expects initial data from CC Explorations to be integrated into its field programs in the coming weeks, with further updates on targeting and drilling plans to follow.

About CC Explorations

CC Explorations ("CCE") brings over two decades of experience in remote sensing for mineral exploration and archaeological services. CCE's proprietary Atomic Mineral Resonance Tomography (AMRT) technology offers a unique, satellite-based method for detecting mineral signatures with exceptional precision. Modelled on techniques used by NASA, AMRT allows for non-invasive, high-resolution, subsurface imaging—positioning CCE at the forefront of next-generation exploration solutions.

Qualified Person

Dr. Scott Jobin-Bevans (P.Geo., PhD), a qualified person (QP) as defined by National Instrument 43-101, has reviewed and approved the scientific and technical disclosure contained within this news release.

About Inspiration Energy Corp.

Inspiration Energy Corp. is engaged in the business of mineral exploration and the acquisition of mineral property assets in Canada. Its objective is to locate and develop properties of merit and to conduct exploration on the Company's properties. The Company holds a portfolio of highly prospective projects in Saskatchewan and other key jurisdictions, with a mission to unlock shareholder value through strategic exploration and development. For more information, please refer to the Company's information available on SEDAR+ (www.sedarplus.ca).

On Behalf of the Board of Directors,

Charles Desjardins

CEO, President and Director

Phone: 604-808-3156

Email: info@inspiration.energy

Neither the Canadian Stock Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this news release.

data.title: Huaneng Power Issues 1.5 Billion Yuan Mid-Term Bonds
data.description:
Huaneng Power International completed the issuance of medium-term bonds amounting to 1.5 billion yuan, according to a Hong Kong bourse filing on Friday.

The bond's interest rate is 1.95% and its maturity period is three or more years.

China Merchants Bank , CITIC Securities , CSC Financial , and China Great Wall Securities acted as the lead underwriters of the issuance.

The proceeds from the issuance will be used to supplement the power company's working capital, adjust its debts structure, as well as repay bank loans and the debt due, the filing said.

Huaneng Power's Hong Kong shares rose less than 2% and Shanghai shares increased more than 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Kunshan Asia Aroma Elects Chair; Shares Up 3%
data.description:
Kunshan Asia Aroma elected Zhou Junxue as chair, according to a Thursday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

Shares of the chemical product manufacturer rose 3% in recent trade.

data.title: French Annual Inflation Eases to 0.7% in May, Final Data Shows
data.description:
As expected, France's annual inflation rate fell to 0.7% in May from 0.8% in April, final data from statistics agency Insee showed Friday.

On a monthly basis, consumer prices were 0.1% lower, consistent with the flash estimate and against the prior 0.6% increase.

The annual harmonized inflation rate declined to 0.6% from 0.9%, matching the preliminary reading. Month over month, harmonized consumer prices edged down 0.2%, as expected, compared with the previous 0.7% jump.

data.title: Yomiuri: Japan's Wholesale Prices Up 3.2% in May
data.description:
By Kazuma Nagahara / Yomiuri Shimbun Staff Writer

Japan's producer price index for May was up 3.2% from the same month last year, with rice prices a driving factor, according to Bank of Japan data. The index stood at 126.3, against a base value of 100 for 2020.

The index, which Japan calls a "corporate goods price index," increased year on year for the 51st straight month, but the growth rate was down from 4.1% in April as crude oil prices continued to fall.

The index indicates changes to the price of goods traded between businesses. Of the 515 items surveyed, 364, or 70%, saw price increases, according to the preliminary data released Wednesday.

Prices rose by 42.8% for "agricultural, forestry and fisheries products," which includes polished rice and brown rice, continuing the strong trend upward from the previous month, when prices grew by 43.5%.

The "beverages and foods" category saw a 4.2% rise, reflecting price increases for raw materials and packaging used for products like boxed lunches. However, "petroleum and coal products" only saw 0.6% growth year on year, down from 6.3% in the previous month.

The yen-based import price index stood at 153.8, marking a 10.3% year-on-year decline.

----

This article is from The Yomiuri Shimbun. Neither Dow Jones Newswires, MarketWatch, Barron's nor The Wall Street Journal were involved in the creation of this content.

YDN-m0000120750-1

data.title: SIA Engineering Target Price Raised to S$3.50 From S$2.80 by DBS Group Research >S59.SG
Japan's Service Sector Activity Rises 0.3% in April
data.description:
Japan's tertiary industry activity index rose 0.3% in April from the previous month, reaching 104.1 on a seasonally adjusted basis, the Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry said on Friday.

Growth was driven by stronger activity in finance and insurance (+3.3%), information and communications (+2.1%), and wholesale trade (+0.8%). Transport and postal services declined 3.4%, weighing on the overall gain.

Compared with a year earlier, the unadjusted index rose 1.9%.

data.title: Brazilian Critical Minerals Records Tracer Reagent in Extraction Holes at Brazil Rare Earths Project
data.description:
Brazilian Critical Minerals recorded the tracer reagent magnesium sulfate in multiple extraction holes over field trial areas undergoing injection at the Ema rare earths project in Brazil, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing

The chemical changes in the solution show that magnesium sulfate migrated from the injection holes to the extraction holes in the field trial locations.

The pH levels reduced significantly in the extraction solution with the addition of low concentration magnesium sulfate and are now at or below pH of 4, which is the level required to leach rare earths into solution via in-situ recovery.

The solutions potentially containing rare earths were extracted from the monitoring holes and are en route to the laboratory for analysis.

data.title: Everest Medicines' Controlling Shareholder to Offload 25 Million Shares
data.description:
Everest Medicines' controlling shareholder, CBC Group, intends to sell 25 million shares in the company via block trades to global long-only and healthcare specialist investors, a Friday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The shares represent around 7.63% of the drug company's issued share capital.

Following the disposal, CBC Group will hold 84,883,427, or 25.92%, shares in the firm.

data.title: Effect on Markets From Israel's Attack on Iran Depends on Scale, Speed of Retaliation — Market Talk
data.description: Israel's attack on nuclear and military targets in Iran has affected oil prices, but its effect on other commodities and markets is so far more limited, Vontobel's Manuel Lang says in a note. "This suggests a wait-and-see approach [regarding] scale, speed, and duration of Iran's retaliation, and whether its attacks will involve U.S. targets," the analyst says. He foresees markets moving to a risk-off mode. Among Swiss stocks, the luxury and travel-related names are likely to be harder hit than others. Transport and logistics disruptions could delay the flow of goods and result in a short-term increase in logistics costs. Defensive stocks and companies with solid balance sheets will weather the storm relatively well, Lang says. Some could even benefit, such as Accelleron due to shipping rerouting, he says. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

data.title: Ecos (India) Mobility Forms Wholly-Owned Fleet Management Subsidiary
data.description:
Car rental and transport services provider Ecos (India) Mobility & Hospitality has incorporated a wholly-owned subsidiary named Ecos Fleet Management Services, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The new company will deal in renting and leasing of motor vehicles and providing vehicle rental solutions.

Eco (India) Mobility subscribed to 10,000 equity shares of 10 rupees each of Ecos Fleet amounting to 100,000 rupees.

The company's shares were down over 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Junshi Biosciences Eyes HK$1.03 Billion from Share Placement
data.description:
Shanghai Junshi Biosciences is looking to raise net proceeds of HK$1.03 billion from the placement of 41 million shares at HK$25.35 apiece to at least six investors, a Friday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The shares represent 15.75% of H shares and 3.99% of total issued shares as enlarged by the allotment and issue.

The Chinese biopharmaceutical company will use proceeds from the placement for innovative drug development and general corporate purposes including replenishment of working capital.

data.title: Lena Åredal Appointed New CEO of SyntheticMR AB (publ)
data.description:
SyntheticMR AB

June 13, 2025

The Board of Directors of SyntheticMR AB (publ) has appointed Lena Åredal as the company’s new Chief Executive Officer. She will assume the position no later than December 15, 2025. Until then, Acting CEO Johanna Norén will continue in her interim role and thereafter return to her position as CFO.

Lena Åredal currently serves as CEO of Perimed, a Swedish medtech company specializing in microcirculation diagnostics with global sales. During her tenure at Perimed, she has been responsible for the company’s OEM partnerships, established a direct sales organization in the United States, restructured its global distribution network, and led the company’s first external capital raise. Previously, Lena held several senior commercial positions at Baxter in the Nordic region, where she built sales organizations and played a key role in multiple acquisition and integration processes. She holds a Master’s degree in Public Health from Karolinska Institutet and has spent her entire career in the Life Science sector.

“In Lena, we have found the leader we have been looking for to guide SyntheticMR through its next phase of development. Her proven commercial drive and ability to build high-performing teams are key strengths as we aim to accelerate the company’s growth. We are entering a phase where, for example, the monitoring of rare but serious adverse drug reactions will drive increased demand for MRI examinations – an area where our technology is uniquely positioned to add value. The Board looks forward to welcoming Lena to the company as soon as possible,” says Gisli Hennermark, Chairman of the Board of SyntheticMR.

For further information, please contact:

Gisli Hennermark, Chairman of the Board, +46 70-420 83 00

Johanna Norén, Acting CEO, +46 70-619 21 00

This disclosure contains information that SyntheticMR AB is obliged to make public pursuant to the EU Market Abuse Regulation (EU nr 596/2014). The information was submitted for publication, through the agency of the contact person, on 13-06-2025 08:56 CET.

SyntheticMR AB (publ) develops innovative imaging solutions that enhance MRI workflow efficiency and diagnostic precision. SyntheticMR’s SyMRI Neuro solution measures brain absolute tissue properties and generates multiple contrast-weighted images, performs automatic segmentation of biomarkers, and delivers quantitative data in a single fast scan. Combinostics, a SyntheticMR company, offers the AI-powered cNeuro suite of products that help clinicians make a difference in the lives of patients with neurological disorders. 

SyntheticMR is headquartered in Linköping, Sweden, with an additional office in Tampere, Finland. For more information, please visit syntheticmr.com and combinostics.com

https://news.cision.com/syntheticmr-ab/r/lena-aredal-appointed-new-ceo-of-syntheticmr-ab--publ-%2Cc4163817

Release

data.title: Hagong Intelligent Robot to Delist on July 11
data.description:
Jiangsu Hagong Intelligent Robot will delist on July 11, following the Shenzhen bourse's ruling due to two consecutive "disclaimer of opinion" audits.

The robotics company's shares will enter a 15-day exit period from June 20, trading under 10% daily limits after the first day, according to a Friday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

After delisting, shares will transfer to the Over-the-Counter market.

data.title: Britain will not protect Israel as Iran retaliates - Times newspaper
data.description: Britain will not protect Israel as Iran retaliates against overnight Israeli strikes, the defence editor of the Times newspaper said on X, without citing sources.

data.title: WTI Rises 6.5% to $72.38 a Barrel
Dollar Lifted by Safe-Haven Demand Due to Middle East Tensions — Market Talk
data.description: The dollar recovers slightly after hitting three-year lows on Thursday. Investors seek safe-haven assets after Israel launched a wide-ranging attack on Iran's nuclear facilities, Pepperstone strategist Michael Brown says in a note. Despite some views that the dollar is no longer acting as a safe haven, "when push comes to shove there is still little else that market participants want to own," he says. The DXY dollar index rises 0.3% to 98.227, having reached a low of 97.602 on Thursday when softer-than-expected U.S. producer prices data boosted bets for interest-rate cuts by the Federal Reserve. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Brent Crude Rises 6.1% to $73.54 a Barrel
